steps.title: Simplify the absolute value function
steps.content: First, simplify the expression inside the absolute value: $x - 4x = -3x$. Therefore, $f(x) = |-3x| = 3|x|$.

steps.title: Evaluate f(5) and set up the equation
steps.content:
Calculate $f(5) = 3|5| = 15$.

Using the given condition $f(5) - f(a) = -15$:
$$15 - f(a) = -15$$
$$f(a) = 30$$

steps.title: Solve for a
steps.content:
Since $f(a) = 3|a|$, we have:
$$3|a| = 30$$
$$|a| = 10$$
This gives $a = 10$ or $a = -10$. Among the answer choices, only $a = 10$ appears. That matches Choice C — $10$.
